      "source": [
        "#**PROJECT OVERVIEW**\n",
        "\n",
        "###UrbanNest Properties has a pricing problem. Their agents estimate house prices manually, which leads to inconsistent valuations, unhappy clients, and lost revenue.\n",
        "###This project uses the California Housing Dataset gotten from Kaggle to build a Linear Regression model that predicts house prices based on data.\n",
        "####Dataset: California Housing Dataset\n",
        "####Source: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/camnugent/california-housing-prices"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "qZKJJBBfWVkE"
      },
      "source": [
        "# **SETUP AND DATA LOADING**"
      ]
    },

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "YhFjmI_Aw-Q7"
      },
      "source": [
        "### **Column details:**\n",
        "\n",
        "1. longitude: A measure of how far west a house is; a higher value is farther west\n",
        "\n",
        "2. latitude: A measure of how far north a house is; a higher value is farther north\n",
        "\n",
        "3. housingMedianAge: Median age of a house within a block; a lower number is a newer building\n",
        "\n",
        "4. totalRooms: Total number of rooms within a block\n",
        "\n",
        "5. totalBedrooms: Total number of bedrooms within a block\n",
        "\n",
        "6. population: Total number of people residing within a block\n",
        "\n",
        "7. households: Total number of households, a group of people residing within a home unit, for a block\n",
        "\n",
        "8. medianIncome: Median income for households within a block of houses (measured in tens of thousands of US Dollars)\n",
        "\n",
        "9. medianHouseValue: Median house value for households within a block (measured in US Dollars)\n",
        "\n",
        "10. oceanProximity: Location of the house w.r.t ocean/sea"
      ]
    },

      "source": [
        "# Frequency of each unique value in the categorical column \"ocean_proximity\"\n",

    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Nh3silxWW2vV"
      },
      "source": [
        "#**EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSIS**"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 472
        },
        "id": "u3-gbzWwDDAz",
        "outputId": "dd65fb26-e4eb-49a0-85ab-7cccd06f2aa9"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ============================================\n",
        "# DISTRIBUTION OF MEDIAN HOUSE VALUE\n",
        "# (UNIVARIATE ANALYSIS)\n",
        "# ============================================\n",
        "\n",
        "# This histogram shows how house prices are distributed.\n",
        "\n",
        "sns.histplot(df['median_house_value'], bins=50,kde=True)\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Median House Value')\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Frequency')\n",
        "plt.title('Distribution of Median House Value')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "pdxs_t04sknB"
      },
      "source": [
        "Most houses fall within the low-to-mid price ranges.\n",
        "\n",
        "The distribution is positively skewed, meaning that there are fewer expensive houses compared to affordable ones."
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 472
        },
        "id": "_IIgcwGInpeD",
        "outputId": "b40604a5-9926-46d8-ba4e-178ee68ccfae"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# =================================================================\n",
        "# DISTRIBUTION OF HOUSES ACROSS DIFFERENT OCEAN PROXIMITY CATEGORY\n",
        "# =================================================================\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.bar(ocean_proximity_count.index, ocean_proximity_count)\n",
        "plt.xlabel(\"Ocean Proximity\")\n",
        "plt.ylabel(\"Frequency\")\n",
        "plt.title(\"Distribution of Houses Across Ocean Proximity Categories\")\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "71jSqepktvtm"
      },
      "source": [
        "Most houses in the dataset are located within less than one hour of the ocean, followed by inland properties. Near ocean and near bay form smaller segments, while island properties are rare.\n",
        "\n",
        "This chart shows that how close houses are to the ocean, is an important way of grouping them but the number of houses in each group also depends on bigger things not just distance from the ocean."
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 654
        },
        "id": "VBeG4OHWEDV4",
        "outputId": "512eefd2-b163-4de3-96ac-81464c0134cd"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 2000x1500 with 9 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "#  OUTLIER ANALYSIS\n",
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "\n",
        "# Defining all the numerical features to check for outliers\n",
        "num_features = [\"longitude\", \"latitude\", \"housing_median_age\",\n",
        "                \"total_rooms\", \"total_bedrooms\", \"population\",\n",
        "                \"households\", \"median_income\", \"median_house_value\"]\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create a 3x3 grid of subplots\n",
        "fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=3, ncols=3, figsize=(20, 15))\n",
        "axes = axes.flatten()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Loop through each numerical features and plot its box plot on the correct subplot\n",
        "for i, col in enumerate(num_features):\n",
        "    sns.boxplot(x=df[col], ax=axes[i])\n",
        "    axes[i].set_title(col, fontsize=15)\n",
        "    axes[i].set_xlabel('')\n",
        "    axes[i].set_ylabel('')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Add a single title to all the box plots\n",
        "plt.suptitle(\"Outlier Check On Numerical Features\")\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Several of the numerical features (total_rooms, total_bedrooms, populations, households, median_income, median_house_value) appears to have outliers beyond the uppper bound."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "L78pksRSZcdA"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 472
        },
        "id": "KW0-758hI1Hy",
        "outputId": "bfc79380-11e7-4f16-a851-95bd74ea0bc9"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "# DISTRIBUTION OF MEDIAN INCOME\n",
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "\n",
        "sns.histplot(df[\"median_income\"],bins=30,kde=True)\n",
        "plt.xlabel(\"Median Income\")\n",
        "plt.ylabel(\"Frequency\")\n",
        "plt.title(\"Distribution of Median Income\")\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "The distribution of median_income is right skewed. Most neighborhoods have moderate income score between 3 and 4. High-income neighbourhood with scores above 8 are very rare."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Wmjx-xCGeltQ"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 472
        },
        "id": "cdBJOw7xMmXe",
        "outputId": "e37c7bb9-5688-4c6b-dafe-e017a4208fb3"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "# OCEAN PROXIMITY VS MEDIAN HOUSE VALUE\n",
        "# (BIVARIATE ANALYSIS)\n",
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "\n",
        "med_house_value = (df.groupby('ocean_proximity')['median_house_value'].mean())\n",
        "plt.bar(med_house_value.index,med_house_value.values)\n",
        "plt.title('Median House Value by Ocean Proximity')\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Ocean Proximity')\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Median House Value')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Properties located closer to the ocean generally have higher average house values. Ocean proximity is a drving force of house value."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "M4urrckHfoNg"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 452
        },
        "id": "hpaQUC9jWZd9",
        "outputId": "85849d9a-9121-4bf6-a8fd-13ba977b07ca"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ============================================\n",
        "# MEDIAN INCOME BY OCEAN PROXIMITY\n",
        "# ============================================\n",
        "\n",
        "# Compare income levels across location categories.\n",
        "\n",
        "income_by_location = (df.groupby('ocean_proximity')['median_income'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False))\n",
        "plt.bar(income_by_location.index,income_by_location.values)\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Median Income')\n",
        "plt.title('Median Income by Ocean Proximity')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Coastal areas generally have higher median incomes, this may explain their higher property values"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "MlKYi8IUL4qE"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 472
        },
        "id": "pZcsXlelRKlD",
        "outputId": "3ea3e54c-bc8d-4c64-c4bd-d0758ab94470"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "# HOUSING AGE VS HOUSE VALUE\n",
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "\n",
        "# Calculate average house value for each housing age\n",
        "age_house_value = (df.groupby('housing_median_age')['median_house_value'].mean())\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create line plot\n",
        "plt.plot(age_house_value.index, age_house_value.values)\n",
        "plt.title('Average House Value by Housing Age')\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Housing Median Age')\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Average House Value')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "9w1NWbKQ6_Gg"
      },
      "source": [
        "The relationship between housing_median_age and media_huose_value is non-linear. While average house value generally increase with age, the pattern is inconsistent. Some older properties especially those around 50+ years show higher average values."
      ]
    },

      "source": [
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "# CORRELATION ANALYSIS\n",
        "# ==========================================\n",
        "\n",
        "# Correlation is computed ONLY for numerical variables.\n",
        "\n",
        "var = ['housing_median_age', 'total_rooms', 'total_bedrooms', 'population', 'households', 'median_income', 'median_house_value']\n",
        "\n",
        "df_corr = df[var].corr()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Display correlation matrix\n",
        "df_corr"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 523
        },
        "id": "yK55dtutfurQ",
        "outputId": "73f67567-97b2-4efa-8cb5-af79a6ed6b84"
      },
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 600x400 with 2 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# Create heatmap\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))\n",
        "sns.heatmap(df_corr, annot=True, cmap='coolwarm')\n",
        "plt.title(\"Correlation Heatmap\")\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "The heatmap shows the strength and direction of relationships between the selected variables.\n",
        "\n",
        "Total bedrooms and households have the strongest positive correlation at 0.97, which suggest that areas with more bedrooms tends to have more households.\n",
        "\n",
        "Housing median age shows a weak positive correlation with median house value at 0.11.\n",
        "\n",
        "Median income shows the strongest correlation with the target at 0.69, which suggests that it will be an important predictor in the house price model."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "FPI8oQc2RS1o"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {

      "source": [
        "plt.scatter(df['median_income'], df['median_house_value'])\n",
        "plt.title('Scatter plot of median income vs median house value')\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Median income')\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Median house value')\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "This shows a strong positive correlation between the median income and the median house value. As the income increases so does the median house value."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "zLc-dhqVoV7S"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "id": "u2fEH-qb6-tc"
      },
      "outputs": [],
      "source": [
        "# Calculating the IQR to understand the spread of values\n",
        "columns= [\"total_rooms\", \"total_bedrooms\", \"population\", \"households\", \"median_income\", \"median_house_value\"]\n",
        "\n",
        "for col in columns:\n",
        "    Q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)\n",
        "    Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)\n",
        "    IQR = Q3 - Q1\n",
        "\n",
        "    upper_bound = Q3 + (1.5 * IQR)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Replacing Values above upper bound with upper bound.\n",
        "    df[col] = np.where(df[col] > upper_bound, upper_bound, df[col])"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "IQR capping was applied to upper outliers to reduce the influence of outliers with high values. Lower bound was skipped because boxplots showed no lower outliers"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "t5V6UR_HVRPD"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "Sh7lE5AVYR89"
      },
      "source": [
        "# **CATEGORICAL ENCODING USING ONE HOT ENCODER**"
      ]
    },

      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {

        },

          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "       <1H OCEAN  INLAND  ISLAND  NEAR BAY  NEAR OCEAN\n",

            "[20640 rows x 5 columns]\n"
          ]
        }
      ],
      "source": [
        "# One-Hot Encode categorical feature ocean_proximity and passing the type as int since Linear Regression requires numeric inputs\n",
        "\n",
        "dummies = pd.get_dummies(df.ocean_proximity)\n",
        "dummies = dummies.astype(int)\n",
        "print(dummies)"
      ]
    },

      "source": [
        "# Droping the ocean_proximity and one of the encoded column to avoid multicollinearity\n",
        "df_final = merged.drop([\"ocean_proximity\", \"INLAND\"],axis=\"columns\")\n",
        "df_final"
      ]
    },

    {
      "cell_type": "code",

        "id": "oRaviKWKeMIW"

      "outputs": [],
      "source": [
        "# Importing train_test_split\n",
        "from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split\n",
        "\n",
        "# Separating features and target\n",
        "X = df_final.drop(columns=['median_house_value'])\n",
        "y = df_final['median_house_value']\n",
        "\n",
        "# Train test split\n",
        "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=42)\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "id": "YSLot6_uUII7"
      },
      "outputs": [],
      "source": [
        "from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler\n",
        "scaler = MinMaxScaler()\n",
        "X_train = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)\n",
        "X_test = scaler.transform(X_test)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Min max scaler was applied to the numerical features sohat all the features have the same 0 to 1 scale and no single feature dominates due to large values."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "NV8rRfiavkco"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": null,
      "metadata": {
        "id": "SSO28f5Fdp-b"
      },
      "outputs": [],
      "source": [
        "from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression\n",
        "model = LinearRegression()"
      ]
    },

      "source": [
        "from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "y_pred = model.predict(X_test)\n",
        "\n",
        "r2 = model.score(X_test, y_test)\n",
        "mae = mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_pred)\n",
        "rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, y_pred))\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f\"R² Score: {r2:.4f}\")\n",
        "print(f\"MAE: ${mae:,.2f}\")\n",
        "print(f\"RMSE: ${rmse:,.2f}\")"
      ]
